svg.title: Redis IAM token lifecycle with credential rotation
svg.desc: Shows the problem window where cached token uses stale AWS credentials after IRSA rotation, and the fix using credential fingerprinting
svg.text: token signed by AKID-1, but creds = AKID-2
cache miss → new token generated immediately
fingerprint = AKID-2 → stored as new entry
When IRSA rotates credentials, accessKeyId changes → different cache key → instant fresh token fetch.
Also proactively refresh at 12 min TTL (not 14) to avoid last-second expiry races.
Each amber bar = 14 min token TTL. Each rotation = new AWS AccessKeyId issued by IRSA/STS.
